summary:
Palpable purpura typically indicates conditions such as Henoch-Schönlein purpura or other vascular diseases involving immunological responses; however, this symptom does not accompany giant cell arteritis which instead presents primarily through tender temporomandibular joints and scalp arteries. The correct answer is \boxed{C}. To keep within two concise sentences while focusing only on critical aspects:

Palpable purpura characterizes several disorders including Henoch-Schönlein purpura but excludes giant cell arteritis where symptoms focus on joint pain and arterial inflammation. Hence, the exception is \boxed{C}.
